steps.title: Complete the square on f(x)
steps.content:
Starting with $f(x) = 5x^2 + 60x + 181$, factor out 5 from the variable terms:
$$f(x) = 5(x^2 + 12x) + 181$$
Complete the square inside the parentheses. Since $(x+6)^2 = x^2 + 12x + 36$, we have $x^2 + 12x = (x+6)^2 - 36$:
$$f(x) = 5((x+6)^2 - 36) + 181 = 5(x+6)^2 - 180 + 181 = 5(x+6)^2 + 1$$
The vertex form reveals that the minimum value of $f$ is 1 (occurring when the squared term equals zero).

steps.title: Apply the composition to find g(x)
steps.content:
Since $g(x) = f(x+8)$ and $f(x) = 5(x+6)^2 + 1$, substitute $(x+8)$ into the vertex form:
$$g(x) = 5((x+8)+6)^2 + 1 = 5(x+14)^2 + 1$$
A horizontal shift of a parabola preserves the minimum value of the function.

Therefore, the minimum of $g$ is also 1, occurring when $(x+14)^2 = 0$.

That matches choice C — C is correct.
